source_pr.title: expression: try to fix TestExprPushDownToFlash tests
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary: So `TestExprPushDownToFlash` failed on my local machine. It is unstable and hard to reproduce. The only questionable code is the failpoint in the `CanPushDownToXXX`, which is enabled by the global `func init()`. I don't know if it is caused by goroutine schedule, forking, race, execution order or whatever.

It is moved into the entry point `TestT`, together with another `func init()`. And all tests passed on my machine, so I think this modification is fine.

FYI, the introduction PR for failpoint and timeutil is #15174, #14826.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note
